head: dense_fg_aux
hypothesis: E-51: Backbone capacity probe — MV3-Small (1.4M) → EfficientNet-B0 (4.6M). EfficientNet uses compound-scaled MBConv blocks with SE modules — architecturally different from MobileNetV3. Prior NaN at lr=1e-3 (E-24, smoke_b1) resolved at lr=1e-4 (E-03). Here lr=3e-4 + 3ep warmup seeks the stability/learning-rate frontier. Core question: does a different CNN architecture family benefit more from dense_fg_aux supervision than MobileNetV3? If pf_mIoU > E-45 (0.8281), EfficientNet's SE-module feature quality may interact positively with dense spatial supervision. If NaN or pf_mIoU < 0.75, EfficientNet architecture is unstable under dense supervision even at reduced LR.
